headlineSummary: Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini are both in negative territory with a close tie and limited evidence, while Anthropic is mixed-to-neutral and OpenAI skews slightly mixed with limited evidence [E1][E2].

ranking.summary: Microsoft Copilot is negative on limited evidence, driven by criticism of its pricing and a user canceling it [E1].
Google Gemini is negative on limited evidence, mainly due to a direct complaint about botched releases [E2].
Anthropic is mixed but leans neutral, with one strong positive note about its AWS viability offset by sharp criticism of its rollout and capacity [E2][E3].
OpenAI is mixed but leans neutral, with praise for Codex and GPT 5.5 offset by a complaint that it was constrained behind Azure [E1][E2].

evidence.excerpt: And this certainly wont bring me back to GitHub Copilot which I cancelled yesterday. GitHub Copilot had competitive pricing until yesterday when they changed from per-request to one of the most expensive per-token quotas. Seriously, take a look at their burning subreddit for some laughs: https://www.reddit.com/r/GithubCopilot I have since changed to DeekSeek Flash on high which is Sonnet+ level for almost free. If I feel I still need smarter models I might signup for $20/mo Codex to use GPT 5.5 which, in my opinion, is the best I can access right now.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names GitHub Copilot, says the user cancelled it, and criticizes its new quota scheme as among the most expensive.
The commenter explicitly cites Codex and GPT 5.5 and says it is 'the best I can access right now,' which is strong praise.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic better get that IPO out soon. Their incredible revenue run-up was basically a result of botched Gemini releases and OpenAI having their hands-tied behind their Azure backs. Anthropic models were quite literally the only viable serverless API (i.e. Bedrock) models on AWS.

annotations.rationale: The comment credits Anthropic with an 'incredible revenue run-up' and calls its models 'quite literally the only viable serverless API' option on AWS.
The phrase 'botched Gemini releases' is a clear, strongly negative judgment about Gemini's releases.
Saying OpenAI had its 'hands-tied behind their Azure backs' criticizes OpenAI as constrained in a way that hurt its competitiveness or availability.

evidence.excerpt: It’s clear that Anthropic has run out of the compute capacity needed to serve Mythos publicly. They’re using security concerns to mask their inability to deliver the model at scale, while still trying to maintain their lead over OpenAI. As a result, they’ve chosen to release it privately under the banner of an “ethical” rollout.

annotations.rationale: The commenter directly accuses Anthropic of lacking capacity and of using security and ethics claims to hide that inability, which is strong criticism of trustworthiness and rollout strategy.
